title: BLDP Management Team and Leadership Transition 2024-2025
text: Ballard Power Systems underwent management transition during 2024-2025 restructuring period. Kate Igbalode became Chief Financial Officer effective September 2024, replacing Paul Dobson. Lee Sweetland became Chief Operating Officer at end of 2024, replacing Mark Biznek. Both departing executives provided transition support to successors. CEO Randy MacEwen led company through challenging 2024 with strategic repositioning toward profitability. New CEO Marty Neese took leadership role in Q4 2025 as company transitioned transformation phase. New management team emphasized cash flow discipline, margin expansion, and commercial term improvements. Leadership changes reflected need for operating-focused executives during restructuring and margin recovery phase. The board maintained strategic direction while supporting management execution on transformation plan. Investor relations communications emphasized disciplined spending, product cost reduction, fleet service monetization, market reach expansion, and innovative business models. Leadership messaging shifted from growth emphasis to profitability path, with management targeting cash flow breakeven by end of 2027. Organizational changes included workforce reductions and span-of-control optimization aligning with operating expense reduction targets.
